summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Transforming the payment experience: How consumers are steering payment innovation
articles.description: Merchants can create enhanced shopping experiences that exceed evolving consumer expectations.

articles.title: Beyond the safari: The realities of managing a global workforce in Africa
articles.description: The real challenge in Africa isn’t hiring - it’s moving money.

articles.title: Walmart brings Sparky to ChatGPT
articles.description: OpenAI is pivoting away from handling purchases after its initial efforts failed to provide enough flexibility.

articles.title: Durbin targets airline points with bill
articles.description: A Senate bill proposed by the Illinois Democrat would regulate airline loyalty programs, with increased disclosure requirements for the carriers.

articles.title: Branch integrates with Stripe for digital wallet payouts
articles.description: The EWA provider is using Stripe’s infrastructure to offer another tool that enables companies and contractors to pay their workforces.

articles.title: Citi pursues payments advances
articles.description: Real-time payments, agentic commerce and stablecoin advances are all topics the bank is discussing with clients, a top executive said.

articles.title: Klarna struggles with loan loss accounting
articles.description: With an assertive push into banking, the buy now, pay later pioneer has expanded consumer lending – along with growth in its loss provisions.

articles.title: FTC threatens major payments players
articles.description: The Federal Trade Commission sent letters to the CEOs of Visa, Mastercard, PayPal and Stripe, saying that denying consumer services for political or religious reasons could prompt enforcement actions.
